originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and lenses scripts, then initializes many inline exposure, optics, wave, and lens sections under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the major interactive sections mount without runtime errors.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with exposure-time detector scenes, drag-driven scene-view handoff, and pinhole-camera controls in which hole diameter and sensor distance update the rendered image.
localStatus: The local route keeps the early exposure and drag scenes, including the detector timing controls, the rotatable scene view, and the pinhole camera's paired diameter and distance controls.
notes: The early scenes still rely on the original slider and drag runtime rather than a local rewrite.
The pinhole sections keep the published inversion, field-of-view, brightness, and sharpness interactions tied to the paired controls.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one exposure slider scene, one early drag scene, and the pinhole dual-slider scene as smoke-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the detector exposure scene redraws from slider input, the early scene responds to drag, and both pinhole sliders update the captured-image scene.

originalBehavior: The published article continues into glass and ray-deflection scenes, animated wave demonstrations with play or pause controls, and later lens and aberration sections with optical sliders.
localStatus: The local route keeps the glass thickness and source-position controls, the draggable glass ray scene, the published wave play/pause path, and the later lens or aberration scenes such as prism and chromatic-aberration controls.
notes: The glass and later lens scenes still rely on the original Drawer runtime and published play/pause controls.
The route keeps the published global wave animation toggle in the article copy rather than replacing it with a local control scheme.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one glass scene, one play/pause control, and one later lens or aberration scene as parity-critical.

Shared smoke coverage verifies a glass-ray slider and drag interaction redraw the active scene, a published play/pause control toggles state, and a later chromatic-aberration slider updates its canvas.

originalBehavior: The published page relies on article-local helper sprites and shared control icons while also exposing author promo links, RSS, and supporter surfaces around the article shell.
localStatus: The local copy vendors the helper sprites locally, keeps the control surfaces intact, and leaves the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The route does not require upstream media requests at runtime beyond the local helper assets and published scripts.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks author-promo, analytics, and supporter surfaces from shipping in the route body.
Shared smoke coverage and route checks verify the route loads locally without upstream asset requests while keeping the interactive controls usable.
